title: Gu-vandi

{{Quote|Besides, master, quite frankly, the Gu-vandi and L'Xing needed a good war. They were races that relied more on words than actions, and a good, brisk killing woke them from their torpid state.|HK-47, to the Jedi Exile|audio=HK-47TheirTorpidState.flac|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
The '''Gu-vandi''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. They were governed by the [[Gu-vandi Collective]], and their area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] included the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Praven Prime]], [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[L'Xing]] species. At some point by [[3951 BBY]], an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]] posing as a [[Translator droid/Legends|translator]] intentionally mistranslated a [[L'Xing (language)|L'Xing]] [[Language/Legends|language]] phrase, causing a [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the Gu-vandi Collective and the L'Xing. As a result of the conflict, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] withdrew its presence from Gu-vandi space.

==Society and culture==
The Gu-vandi were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of which members were governed by the [[Gu-vandi Collective]]. According to the [[HK-series assassin droid]] [[HK-47]], the Gu-vandi had a "torpid" disposition that led them to favor words over actions.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==Gu-vandi in the galaxy==
{{Quote|Recitation: For example, on Praven Prime, the simple transferring of L'Xing syntax for 'friendship' changes its meaning—and implies that one's brood mate was actually impregnated by their own host. ''[…]'' Statement: This comment, of course, caused a civil war between the Gu-vandi Collective and L'Xing that still persists to the current date.|HK-47|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
[[File:PravenPrime-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Gu-vandi Collective included Praven Prime.]]
By some point between [[3955 BBY]] and [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="Dates">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', which establishes that the [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the [[Gu-vandi Collective]] and the [[L'Xing]] broke out no more than four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, to [[3951 BBY]].</ref> the Gu-vandi controlled an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that included the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Praven Prime]]<ref name="KOTOR2" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Quelii sector/Legends|Quelii sector]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Praven Prime system/Legends|Praven Prime system]]</ref>—the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the sentient [[L'Xing]] species and a part of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. At one point, an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]]<ref name="KOTOR2" />—a unit of an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] model that had originally been designed by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] and was later [[HK Manufacturing Plant|mass-produced]] by the rogue [[G0-T0 infrastructure planning system]] known as [[G0-T0]]<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>—posing as a [[Translator droid/Legends|translator droid]] intentionally mistranslated the word for "friendship" in the [[L'Xing (language)|L'Xing]] [[Language/Legends|language]] to instead convey the implication that a specific individual's [[Reproduction/Legends|brood]] mate had been impregnated by their own host. The translated comment sparked a [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the L'Xing and the Gu-vandi Collective. As a result of the civil war, the Republic decided to withdraw control over Praven Prime and retreated from Gu'vandi space.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}}
[[File:HK-47.png|thumb|right|180px|HK-47 (pictured) claimed the civil war had a positive impact on the Gu-vandi.]]
In 3951 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="Dates" /> while the civil war was still ongoing, HK-47,<ref name="KOTOR2" /> who had originally served as the basis for the HK-50 model,<ref name="KOTORCG" /> informed the Human [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] known as the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] about the conflict involving the Gu-vandi. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] reasoned that the outbreak of the conflict and the withdrawal of the Republic would eventually prove to be beneficial to that government, speculating that continued control over the world would have been an unnecessary burden in the form of resources needed to maintain [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] and [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] relations. HK-47 further claimed that the Gu-vandi had "needed a good war" and that the conflict had "woken" the species from its relatively inactive disposition.<ref name="KOTOR2" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Gu-vandi were introduced in the [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. In the game, the species can be mentioned in an optional conversation the player character, the Jedi Exile, can have with the character HK-47. This article assumes one hundred percent game completion and that the conversation took place as described.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1stm}}
